title: Coherent categorification of quantum loop sl(2)
speaker: Peng Shan
affiliation: Tsinghua University; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: We explain an equivalence of categories between a module category of quiver Hecke algebras associated with the Kronecker quiver and a category of equivariant perverse coherent sheaves on the nilpotent cone of type A. This provides a link between two different monoidal categorifications of the open quantum unipotent cell of affine type A1A1, one given by Kang-Kashiwara-Kim-Oh-Park in terms of quiver Hecke algebras, the other given by Cautis-Williams in terms of equivariant perverse coherent sheaves on affine Grassmannians.
tags: Virtual Workshop on Recent Developments in Geometric Representation Theory

proof_stages.label: Classical geometric and KLR categorifications of U_q(n)
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls Lusztig's constructible-sheaf convolution category on quiver representation stacks, its Grothendieck group and canonical basis, and the equivalent quiver-Hecke/KLR algebraic categorification.

proof_stages.label: Coherent cotangent-stack categorification of the quantum loop algebra
proof_stages.summary: Replacing constructible sheaves on representation stacks by coherent sheaves on derived cotangent stacks gives a convolution category whose Grothendieck group is the quantum enveloping algebra of the loop algebra, motivating a comparison with affine KLR categorifications.

proof_stages.label: sl2 specialization and the common Drinfeld–Kashiwara positive part
proof_stages.summary: For the one-vertex sl2 quiver the coherent category becomes equivariant coherent sheaves on \mathfrak{gl}_r, and comparison with affine sl2 roots identifies the shared subalgebra generated by roots \beta_n=\alpha_0+n\delta, namely U_q(n[t]).

proof_stages.label: Affine-sl2 KLR subcategory generated by real-root simples
proof_stages.summary: Choosing the Kronecker orientation and a convex order produces dual root vectors and corresponding simple KLR modules L(\beta_n), whose monoidal Serre subcategory C categorifies U_q(n[t]).

proof_stages.label: Main equivalence with perverse coherent sheaves on the nilpotent cone
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem constructs an equivalence between a central-character specialization C^\sharp of the KLR category and equivariant perverse coherent sheaves on nilpotent cones, preserving Grothendieck groups, proper standards, and a weak monoidal structure, with a conjectural undeformed derived version over all \mathfrak{gl}_r.

proof_stages.label: Application to monoidal cluster categorification
proof_stages.summary: Known KLR and Cautis–Williams coherent categorifications of quantum unipotent-cell cluster algebras are compared via the main theorem and a flat morphism from affine-Grassmannian strata to nilpotent cones, yielding a graded monoidal faithful functor and a conjectural localized equivalence.

examples.name: KLR categorifications of related quantum-group structures
examples.note: The speaker briefly cites categorifications of integrable representations and of U_q(g) as illustrations of why the algebraic quiver-Hecke model is useful beyond Lusztig's original categorification.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker develops the KLR/quiver-Hecke categorification in detail, defining the algebras and module categories, their induction product and Grothendieck groups, then specializing to affine \(\mathfrak{sl}_2\) and constructing subcategories to compare with coherent sheaves.

passes.reason: KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications form a systematic standard family used as motivating comparisons, not as failures of any claim.
KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications are invoked as a standard systematic family of algebraic categorifications motivating the construction, not as a failure case.
KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications are a systematic family used as standard comparison and motivation for the coherent categorification, not as a failure case.
KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications are a systematic family of algebraic categorifications used as the comparison target for coherent categorification, not to refute a claim.

KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications are presented as a systematic standard family of algebraic categorifications used for quantum-group structures, not as sporadic, degenerate, extremal, maximally symmetric, or as refuting any claim.
Systematic KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications of quantum-group structures are ordinary family constructions used to illustrate algebraic usefulness, not to refute a claim.
A systematic family of KLR/quiver-Hecke algebra categorifications used illustratively to show the algebraic approach applies to many quantum-group structures, not to refute a claim.

KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications are used as standard systematic categorifications and comparison objects for quantum-group structures, not to refute a claim or as structurally sporadic/extreme cases.
KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications are a systematic standard family used as background and comparison, not to refute a claim.
A standard systematic family of quiver-Hecke/KLR categorifications used as contextual motivation rather than to refute a claim.

KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications are used as a systematic algebraic framework for quantum-group structures, not to refute a claim, and they are a broad family rather than structurally exceptional, extremal, symmetric, or degenerate.
KLR/quiver-Hecke categorifications are presented as a systematic family of algebraic categorifications of quantum-group structures, not as an obstruction or structurally sporadic case.
